objects.name: Analytic 1719
objects.description: From the defender view: an app registers a clipboard listener or calls ClipboardManager getters; the app is (a) foreground, (b) the default IME, or (c) abusing legacy paths. Shortly after each clipboard change, the app reads the primary clip repeatedly, optionally persists content (local file/DB) and/or exfiltrates it. We correlate: listener/clip-access → privilege/foreground confirmation → bursty reads → local write and/or network egress within a tight window.

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: android:logcat
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: Activity/Process state change (mFocusedApp, onResume/onPause) identifying <pkg> as foreground

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: android:logcat
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: Default IME active or bound to <pkg> (InputMethodManager reports imeId=<pkg>)

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: android:logcat
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: CREATE/WRITE to app-writable DB/file path indicating clipboard dump (e.g., clipboard.db, clip_*.txt)

x_mitre_mutable_elements.description: Max time between clip access → persist/exfil (e.g., 5–45s).
Minimum reads per clipboard change to flag harvesting (e.g., ≥2).
Regex for files/DBs used to stash clipboard content in app container.
Allowlisted domains to suppress false positives for analytics SDKs.
Require foreground unless app is the default IME (true/false).
Work Profile/Developer Mode/Doze to scope alerts.
